title: smoky, fluffy, monsoon vietnamese chicken pho-ga
description: A smoky, fluffy, and comforting Vietnamese Chicken Noodle Broth, perfect for monsoon season. This dish features shio-koji-cured chicken, Chinese broccoli, and a rich, savory broth.
instructions: In a large stockpot, combine chicken bones, onion, ginger, garlic, fish sauce, soy sauce, and sugar. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 1 hour, skimming any impurities that rise to the surface.
Add star anise and cinnamon sticks to the pot and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.
While the broth is simmering, cure the chicken thighs with shio-koji according to package instructions.
When the broth is ready, remove the star anise and cinnamon sticks and strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot.
Add the cured chicken thighs to the pot and cook until heated through.
Prepare the noodles according to package instructions and divide among bowls.
Slice the Chinese broccoli into florets and add to the bowls.
Slice the cured chicken thighs into thin strips and add to the bowls.
Pour the hot broth over the noodles, chicken, and broccoli.
Season with black pepper to taste and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
